question: Will Kevin Roy finish in the Top 20 at the 2026 Valspar Championship?
description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed player finishes in the top 20 at the 2026 Valspar Championship tournament, including ties. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". eventMetadata.context_description: Peter Malnati's surprising victory at last year's Valspar Championship underscores the Copperhead Course's bias toward precise iron play and scrambling, key factors shaping current Top 20 trader consensus for this week's event. No major withdrawals reported from top favorites like Rory McIlroy or Jordan Spieth, though McIlroy's inconsistent Innisbrook history tempers his implied probabilities despite hot recent form. Keith Mitchell, runner-up in 2024, leads value plays with strong course stats, while calm weather forecasts favor grinders over bombers. Schedule rest advantages for FedEx Cup leaders add momentum, but the field's depth means upsets remain likely per historical volatility.

question: ChatGPT Full Outage by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if OpenAI's ChatGPT experiences any incident classified as 'Full Outage' as of the time it is marked as “Resolved” by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only incidents listing ChatGPT under 'Affected components' will be considered. Incidents labeled as affecting 'APIs,' or 'Sora,' but not ChatGPT, will have no bearing on the resolution of this market.

Classifications of an incident while it is ongoing will have no bearing on the resolution of this market. Only classifications of events that are resolved will be considered.

Qualifying incidents include outages and other issues classified as 'Full Outage' when they are resolved, during this market's above-specified timeframe. 

An incident resolved outside this market’s timeframe will only qualify if ongoing at this market’s resolution time, in which case the market will remain open until that incident is marked as “Resolved,” and resolution will be based on the first impact classification thereafter, regardless of subsequent revisions or corrections.

Revisions that upgrade an incident’s impact classification to 'Full Outage' will qualify if the incident was resolved and the revision is published within this market’s timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official system status information published by OpenAI on status.openai.com; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' near-unanimous 95.3% implied probability on "No" for a full ChatGPT outage by March 31 reflects OpenAI's battle-tested infrastructure on Microsoft Azure, which has scaled to billions of queries without total collapse. Late-2024 degradations, like the December 12 partial downtime, resolved in hours, underscoring improved redundancy post-GPT-4o rollout. No systemic red flags appear in recent status logs or filings, with historical outages limited to regional or feature-specific hiccups. Slim risks persist: a sophisticated DDoS attack, GPU supply disruptions from chip shortages, or edge-case regulatory probes forcing emergency halts—though traders dismiss these amid OpenAI's proactive monitoring.

question: Will Trump talk to Masoud Pezeshkian in March?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual talks with Donald Trump between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

A talk is defined as any interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Who will Trump talk to in March?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual talks with Donald Trump between March 1 and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

A talk is defined as any interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's "Who will Trump talk to in March?" market favors Lex Fridman at around 45% implied probability, driven by Trump's recent pattern of engaging high-profile podcasters post-election and Fridman's outreach via X posts confirming mutual interest. Key catalysts include Trump's December 2024 Joe Rogan interview boosting his media strategy visibility and Fridman's appeal to tech-savvy audiences aligning with Trump's Silicon Valley ties. Other contenders like Patrick Bet-David (25%) and Theo Von (20%) reflect speculation from unconfirmed scheduling teases, but no official announcements have materialized. Uncertainty persists ahead of Trump's inauguration on January 20, 2025, with potential shifts from campaign trail hints or direct statements; watch for mid-February updates that could sway odds.

question: Will "The Super Mario Galaxy Movie" Opening Weekend Box Office be less than 160m?
description:
This market will resolve according to how much "The Super Mario Galaxy Movie" Opening Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its opening weekend. The "Daily" figures found on the “Domestic Daily” tab on this movie's Box Office Mojo (https://www.boxofficemojo.com/title/tt28650488/) page will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 5-day opening weekend (April 1 - April 5) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the BoxOfficeMojo figures provided under Domestic Daily performance for the 5-day weekend, regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is ambiguity as to whether the resolution source's figures are final, this market will remain open until both https://www.boxofficemojo.com/ and https://www.the-numbers.com/ have confirmed their finalized figures.

If there is no final data available by April 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for the Super Mario Galaxy Movie's opening weekend box office tilts toward a robust $180-200 million domestic haul, with implied probabilities peaking at 28% for over $200 million amid fervent Nintendo fandom and Illumination's proven animation pedigree from the 2023 Super Mario Bros. smash ($146 million debut). Recent trailer drops showcasing Galaxy's cosmic gameplay nostalgia have fueled pre-sale surges, but close odds across top bins reflect caution over sequel fatigue, star power continuity (Pratt, Key voicing), and stiff competition from concurrent family tentpoles like potential Disney releases. Critical previews and Thursday previews will be pivotal differentiators, as historical IP sequels like Minions: The Rise of Gru hovered near $100 million before exploding.

question: Will a U.S. anti-cartel operation outside of the United States occur by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if U.S. government personnel (military, DEA, CIA, or any other agency) directly participate on the ground in an anti-cartel operation or conduct a kinetic strike directed against a cartel on foreign soil by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

U.S. personnel must directly participate to qualify. U.S. personnel involved in intelligence, surveillance, logistical, support, or advisory roles will not count.

Only direct U.S. participation, confirmed by the U.S. Government or by an overwhelming consensus of reporting, will count. For example, previous operations such as the 2014 capture of Joaquín "El Chapo" Guzmán, in which U.S. forces were rumored to have been embedded with Mexican Marines, would not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the U.S. government; however, an overwhelming consensus of reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the agency leading a U.S. anti-cartel operation abroad hinges on President-elect Trump's repeated pledges for aggressive action against Mexican cartels, including potential military strikes, following his November 2024 election win. DEA remains the frontrunner in implied probabilities, reflecting its longstanding lead role in extraterritorial narcotics enforcement via operations like those in Mexico and Central America, bolstered by recent captures of Sinaloa leaders. Speculation rose after Trump's nomination of military figures like Pete Hegseth for Defense Secretary, potentially elevating special forces options, though Mexico's diplomatic pushback tempers invasion odds. Key upcoming events include Hegseth's confirmation hearings in January 2025 and bilateral talks with President Sheinbaum, which could clarify operational scope and shift crowd wisdom.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the agency leading a U.S. anti-cartel operation abroad hinges on President-elect Trump's repeated pledges for aggressive action against Mexican cartels, including potential military strikes, following his November 2024 election win. DEA remains the frontrunner in implied probabilities, reflecting its longstanding lead role in extraterritorial narcotics enforcement via operations like those in Mexico and Central America, bolstered by recent captures of Sinaloa leaders. Speculation rose after Trump's nomination of military figures like Pete Hegseth for Defense Secretary, potentially elevating special forces options, though Mexico's diplomatic pushback tempers invasion odds. Key upcoming events include Hegseth's confirmation hearings in January 2025 and bilateral talks with President Sheinbaum, which could clarify operational scope and shift crowd wisdom.

question: Spread: Randers FC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Denmark Superliga game, scheduled for April 12 at 12:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Randers FC" if Randers FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC København".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on superligaen.dk. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Pumas de la UNAM vs. Mazatlán FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Liga MX game, scheduled for Sunday, April 12, 2026 between Pumas de la UNAM and Mazatlán FC.

question: Will Leavitt say "Regime" 10+ times during the next White House Press Briefing?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the acting White House Press Secretary says the listed term during the next White House press briefing they participate in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated content will not be considered when resolving this market.

If no such press briefing happens by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market refers to White House press briefings by the acting White House Press Secretary (https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/topic/calendar/). Any press briefing by the acting White House Press Secretary will qualify. "New Media" press briefings or special briefings such as those including extra guests will be considered, so long as the acting White House Press Secretary  is a listed participant. Impromptu press gaggles will not be considered for the resolution of this market; only events listed on the public schedule as briefings or announcements will qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be footage of the relevant briefing.

question: Spread: Seattle Sounders FC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming MLS game, scheduled for April 12 at 9:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Seattle Sounders FC" if Seattle Sounders FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Real Salt Lake".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on mlssoccer.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Seattle Sounders FC vs. Real Salt Lake - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for April 12 at 9:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Seattle Sounders hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their home clash with Real Salt Lake, driven by Lumen Field's fortress status—undefeated in last five meetings there—and recent form showing three wins in four Western Conference games. Sounders' injury report lists midfielder João Paulo as questionable with a hamstring strain, but key attackers like Jordan Morris are fit after international break recovery; RSL counters with star Chicho Arango questionable (ankle knock from midweek), potentially disrupting their attack that ranks top-three in goals. Momentum favors Seattle post-2-0 Minnesota win, while RSL's road woes (winless in last four away) and tougher schedule ahead amplify home/rest advantages shaping implied probabilities.

This market will resolve to "Perth Glory FC" if Perth Glory FC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Macarthur FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on a-league.com.au. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Perth Glory FC vs. Macarthur FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Australia A-League game, scheduled for April 12 at 5:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Perth Glory's defensive vulnerabilities remain the pivotal factor tilting trader sentiment toward caution in their A-League home matchup against Macarthur FC, conceding 2.3 goals per game lately amid a six-match winless streak. Glory's rest advantage after a lighter schedule contrasts Macarthur's road woes (winless in five away), but both teams' mid-table scrambles heighten upset potential. Historical head-to-heads in Perth favor Glory slightly, reflecting crowd wisdom on implied probabilities.

events.title: Melbourne City FC vs. Wellington Phoenix FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Australia A-League game, scheduled for April 12 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Melbourne City's unbeaten home streak across nine A-League matches anchors trader consensus favoring them against Wellington Phoenix, who face a tough road test amid a three-game winless skid away from New Zealand. City tops the ladder with clinical finishing from attackers like Yamashita, bolstered by a full squad per latest reports, while Phoenix grapples with midfielder Kosta Barbarouses' doubtful status from a hamstring tweak. Head-to-head favors City 4-1 in recent clashes, and their rest advantage post-midweek bye sharpens focus against Phoenix's travel fatigue. Momentum tilts toward a City victory, though Phoenix's counterattacking threat keeps upset potential alive in this playoff-pivotal Round 22 clash.

question: Will the Emmanuel Grégoire List win the most citywide list votes in the runoff of the 2026 Paris municipal election by 20%+?
description:
The second round of the 2026 Paris municipal election is scheduled to be held on March 22, 2026. Paris uses a two-round proportional list system with a majority bonus. Voters cast two ballots: one for a citywide list that determines the composition of the Council of Paris, and one for a list in their arrondissement that determines the arrondissement councils. The members of the Council of Paris subsequently elect the Mayor of Paris. This market is based solely on the list vote totals for the entire city of Paris, with lists identified by their lead candidate.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two lists based on the total number of votes received in the citywide list vote in the second round of this election.

For the purposes of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid citywide list votes received by the list that wins the most citywide list votes and the list that wins the second-most citywide list votes. Percentages of the valid votes received by each list will be determined by dividing the total number of valid citywide list votes each of the top two lists receives by the sum of all valid citywide list votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve solely based on citywide list votes cast in the second round of this election. Votes cast for arrondissement lists will not be considered.

If two or more lists tie for the most valid citywide list votes in this election, and the tie is between the two listed lists, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the list whose listed lead candidate's name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed list and an unlisted list, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed list. If the tie is between two or more unlisted lists, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If any unlisted list wins the most citywide list votes in this election, or the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Paris mayoral election runoff heavily favors Emmanuel Grégoire securing a 5–15% victory margin over Rachida Dati, driven by recent polling averages from IFOP and Elabe showing him ahead 52–46% in head-to-head matchups, implying a 6–10% edge after accounting for undecideds and turnout dynamics. Grégoire benefits from Socialist Party continuity as interim mayor, bolstered by left-wing endorsements post-Anne Hidalgo's retirement, while Dati's conservative momentum from her culture minister role has stalled amid Macron government fatigue. Uncertainty lingers with potential leftist vote splits or right-wing consolidations, but no major catalysts have shifted odds recently, with traders pricing in a tight race absent surprises before the 2026 vote.

question: Will a U.S. anti-cartel operation outside of the United States occur by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if U.S. government personnel (military, DEA, CIA, or any other agency) directly participate on the ground in an anti-cartel operation or conduct a kinetic strike directed against a cartel on foreign soil by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

U.S. personnel must directly participate to qualify. U.S. personnel involved in intelligence, surveillance, logistical, support, or advisory roles will not count.

Only direct U.S. participation, confirmed by the U.S. Government or by an overwhelming consensus of reporting, will count. For example, previous operations such as the 2014 capture of Joaquín "El Chapo" Guzmán, in which U.S. forces were rumored to have been embedded with Mexican Marines, would not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the U.S. government; however, an overwhelming consensus of reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the agency leading a U.S. anti-cartel operation abroad hinges on President-elect Trump's repeated pledges for aggressive action against Mexican cartels, including potential military strikes, following his November 2024 election win. DEA remains the frontrunner in implied probabilities, reflecting its longstanding lead role in extraterritorial narcotics enforcement via operations like those in Mexico and Central America, bolstered by recent captures of Sinaloa leaders. Speculation rose after Trump's nomination of military figures like Pete Hegseth for Defense Secretary, potentially elevating special forces options, though Mexico's diplomatic pushback tempers invasion odds. Key upcoming events include Hegseth's confirmation hearings in January 2025 and bilateral talks with President Sheinbaum, which could clarify operational scope and shift crowd wisdom.

question: North Korea missile test/launch by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if North Korea (DPRK) launches a missile between market creation and the specified date ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only launches of ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and anti-ship missiles will qualify toward a “Yes” resolution.

Launches of other systems such as SAMs, MLRS/rocket artillery, torpedoes, etc., will not qualify toward a "Yes" resolution.

The market resolution will be based on publicly available information from reliable sources such as official government statements, reports from international monitoring bodies like the United Nations, or reports from reputable international media.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favoring no North Korean missile test or launch by March 31 stems from Pyongyang's relative restraint in March, with no confirmed ballistic or cruise missile firings reported by South Korean or US military monitors. Earlier 2024 tests, including January cruise missiles, have not escalated recently amid stable Korean Peninsula dynamics, US-South Korea drills winding down, and no major diplomatic catalysts. Markets reflect this lull, pricing low risk of a pre-deadline launch despite DPRK's unpredictable patterns.

question: Bab el-Mandeb Strait effectively closed by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF PortWatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait less than or equal to 10 for any date between market creation and the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF PortWatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait equal to or below 10, or once data has been published for the listed date and no such value has been published.

If no data has been published for the listed date within 14 calendar days (ET) after that date, this market will resolve based on the data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made before data has been published for the listed date will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions made after data has been published for the listed date will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF PortWatch, specifically the “Arrivals of Ships” data published for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/6b1814d64903461b98144a6cc25eb79c
, including both the chart and downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Houthi rebel attacks on commercial shipping have driven Polymarket's implied probability of an effective Bab el-Mandeb Strait closure to around 18%, reflecting trader consensus on escalating Red Sea risks amid the Israel-Hamas conflict, though U.S.-led coalition airstrikes have prevented total shutdown. Verified data shows Suez Canal revenues down 50% year-over-year to $5.6 billion through February, with Asia-Europe container freight rates surging 250% to $4,000 per 40-foot unit per Drewry index, embedding a 5-10% oil risk premium into Brent crude at $82/barrel. Traders eye upcoming U.S. Navy operations and potential UN-mediated Yemen talks as pivotal, with full closure requiring sustained 90%+ traffic diversion—currently at 75% per UNCTAD—potentially adding 0.7% to global CPI via supply chain strains.

question: California voter ID referendum passes?
description:
The California voter ID initiative is a proposed statewide ballot measure for the California general election currently scheduled for November 3, 2026, which would require voters to present government-issued identification in California elections.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the voter identification initiative proposed by Reform California or any other statewide ballot measure that establishes a voter ID requirement for voting in California elections is approved by a majority of voters at the California statewide general election currently scheduled for November 3, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

If by the last legally operative deadline for placing measures on the November 3, 2026 statewide ballot, including any deadline set or modified by statute governing the initiative qualification process, no qualifying voter identification initiative has been approved for submission to voters, and there is no enacted statute or pending court order that could still place such a measure on that ballot, the market will resolve “No”.

If the election is officially rescheduled, the same rule applies to the new ballot and its corresponding deadlines.

The resolution source will be based on the official certified results of the vote as published by the California Secretary of State. Subsequent litigation, administrative challenges, or failure of any post-approval triggers will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Slow progress in collecting the 546,651 signatures needed to qualify a Republican-backed Voter ID ballot initiative for California's November 2026 election anchors trader consensus at 78% against passage. As of late October 2024, the California GOP reported only around 20,000 signatures amid logistical hurdles and limited volunteer turnout, with the filing deadline over 18 months away but historical precedents showing frequent failures for similar measures. While polls indicate 60-70% public support for voter photo ID requirements across party lines, Democratic opposition and high qualification barriers in a blue state weigh heavily on odds. No immediate catalysts loom, leaving markets focused on signature drive momentum.

eventMetadata.context_description: Llaneros FC holds a razor-thin 52% implied probability edge over AD Cali at 51%, with draw odds equally tight at 50%, reflecting evenly matched Primera B contenders locked in a mid-table scrap. Head-to-head history favors stalemates, with two draws and a Llaneros win in the last three, keeping trader sentiment balanced amid unpredictable Colombian second-division volatility.

events.title: SD Eibar vs. UD Las Palmas - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for March 29 at 8:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Eibar's strong home form at Ipurua has traders leaning toward the hosts in this Copa del Rey clash, with recent Segunda Division dominance including four straight home wins fueling optimism. Las Palmas, mired in La Liga relegation peril with just one victory in their last 10 matches overall, face rotation risks amid a congested schedule against a rested Eibar side. Key absences include Eibar's suspended midfielder Arbilla, but Las Palmas' injury-hit defense—missing Viera and McBurnie—amplifies vulnerabilities. Head-to-head history favors Eibar (2-1 aggregate last two meetings), and trader consensus reflects this edge amid Las Palmas' poor away scoring (0.8 goals per game). Schedule fatigue could tip momentum further.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vejle BK's potent attack clashes with FC Fredericia's stubborn home defense in this Danish 1st Division matchup, fueling the razor-thin trader consensus across all outcomes at 49.5%. Vejle, freshly relegated from the Superliga and sitting second with a four-match unbeaten streak including road wins, holds edge in quality, but Fredericia's recent form—two wins and two draws in five—plus a perfect home record against mid-table foes keeps it competitive. No major injuries reported from official updates, head-to-head splits even (two draws last season), and both sides' high-stakes positioning amplify uncertainty, reflecting crowd wisdom in the bunched probabilities.

question: Will FC Fredericia vs. Vejle BK end in a draw?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 13, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Will the DFM Real Estate Index hit 18,000 in 2026?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any TradingView 1 minute candle for the DFM Real Estate Index between the creation of this market and December 31, 2026, 23:59 in the ET timezone has a final "High" value equal to or greater than the value specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is TradingView, specifically the DFM Real Estate Index "High" values available at https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=DFM%3ADFMREI, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the data from the DFM Real Estate Index chart. Values from other exchanges or different indexes will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

events.title: What level will the Dubai Real Estate Index hit in 2026?
events.description: What level will the Dubai Real Estate Index hit in 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Dubai's real estate market sentiment tilts bullish for 2026, with traders pricing in sustained index growth above 200 points amid robust foreign investment inflows exceeding $20 billion in H1 2024 per Dubai Land Department data. Primary drivers include population growth from golden visas—over 100,000 issued last year—and supply shortages pushing villa prices up 25% YoY, while apartment values rose 18%. UAE's non-oil GDP expansion at 4.4% supports demand, though global rate cuts and oil volatility above $70/barrel pose risks. Key watch: Q4 2024 master plan updates and 2025 Expo momentum, with historical precedents like post-2020 rebound implying 15-20% upside potential by year-end. Market-implied odds reflect trader capital betting on diversification tailwinds over cyclical corrections.

question: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by April 30, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns low implied probability to Kharg Island—the Persian Gulf's vital Iranian oil export hub—losing Tehran's control by the resolution date, reflecting its firm operational status amid Israel-Iran shadow war tensions. Recent Israeli airstrikes in October 2024 hit fuel depots near Tehran but bypassed Kharg, while Houthi threats to Gulf shipping lanes have not disrupted Iranian oversight there. No verified intelligence or official reports signal imminent challenges to sovereignty, prioritizing the island's economic lifeline role. Key risks stem from escalation in proxy conflicts or direct reprisals, with traders monitoring U.S. diplomatic signals and IAEA nuclear updates for potential market movers.

question: Major solar storm by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a solar radiation storm with a severity level of 3 or greater occurs between market creation and April 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

An S3 event is a solar radiation storm with a "Strong" classification. More information on the space weather classification criteria can be found here: https://www.swpc.noaa.gov/noaa-scales-explanation

This market will resolve immediately upon the occurrence of a qualifying event. Any later revisions or corrections will not be considered.

This market will resolve based on information from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration's Space Weather Prediction Center under the heading marked "Current Space Weather Conditions". (https://www.swpc.noaa.gov/products/alerts-watches-and-warnings#)

If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve to "No".

events.title: Major solar storm by April 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a solar radiation storm with a severity level of 3 or greater occurs between market creation and April 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve immediately upon the occurrence of a qualifying event. Any later revisions or corrections will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center's latest 3-day and 27-day outlooks show low probabilities for G4+ geomagnetic storms through April 30, with no Earth-directed coronal mass ejections (CMEs) from recent C-class flares, anchoring the 83.5% "No" odds on a major solar storm. Solar Cycle 25 remains active but subdued currently, featuring stable active regions like AR3664 producing only minor flares, far below the X-class events needed for severe impacts. Trader consensus reflects this quiet baseline—historical April data shows rare major storms (under 10% incidence)—tempered by inherent forecast uncertainty, though upcoming solar wind data releases could shift sentiment if unexpected activity emerges.

question: Will "Ready or Not 2: Here I Come" Opening Weekend Box Office be less than 7m?
description:
This market will resolve according to how much "Ready or Not 2: Here I Come" Opening Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its opening weekend. The "Daily" figures found on the “Domestic Daily” tab on this movie's Box Office Mojo (https://www.boxofficemojo.com/title/tt28650488/) page will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day opening weekend (March 20 - March 22) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the BoxOfficeMojo figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend (which typically includes Thursday's previews), regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the BoxOfficeMojo figures provided under Domestic Daily performance for the 3-day weekend, regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is no final data available by March 29, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders overwhelmingly back an $8.5-10 million opening weekend for Ready or Not 2: Here I Come, with 97.7% market-implied odds, propelled by robust pre-sale tracking mirroring the original's $8.2 million debut as a horror-comedy sleeper hit. Recent trailer drops have amassed 15 million views, fueling buzz around Samara Weaving's return and the franchise's twisted bridal-game premise, while October's lighter genre competition aids visibility. Historical precedents like Happy Death Day 2U (similar $9.4M open) bolster this consensus. Upsets could stem from review embargo lifts yielding mixed scores, rainy weather curbing walk-ups, or unexpected streamer counterprogramming siphoning young adult audiences.
